---
title: "Exemplars"
---

Profiles of real works, each read as a consistent stat block and cross-linked to
the mechanisms it shows off. Where the species accounts describe a technique in
the abstract, an exemplar shows it doing its job in a shipped game or experience.
Follow a profile's links to reach the articles it illustrates; follow an
article's *Referenced by* register to find the works that demonstrate it.

## Tabletop

- [Apocalypse World](apocalypse-world.qmd): moves and the fiction-mechanic loop, playbooks, and the MC as a designed role.
- [Blades in the Dark](blades-in-the-dark.qmd): position-and-effect resolution, progress clocks, the flashback, and faction clocks as a world-engine.
- [Blood on the Clocktower](blood-on-the-clocktower.qmd): social deduction without player elimination, run by a Storyteller who cannot play.
- [Call of Cthulhu](call-of-cthulhu.qmd): the Sanity mechanic and investigative cluing, with horror encoded in the rules.
- [Dungeons & Dragons, 5th Edition](dungeons-and-dragons-5e.qmd): the d20 core and bounded accuracy, an accessible on-ramp, and the DM's wide authority.
- [Exit: The Game](exit-the-game.qmd): a boxed escape game you write on and destroy, self-checked by a decoder dial.
- [Fiasco](fiasco.qmd): GM-less play where a dice-built web of relationships and needs drives ordinary people to ruin across two acts.
- [Ironsworn](ironsworn.qmd): the oracle as a solo resolution engine and progress tracks, in a game built to run with or without a GM.
- [Magic: the Gathering](magic-the-gathering.qmd): deck construction as a metagame, the colour pie, and the living, rotating format that founded the TCG.
- [Pandemic](pandemic.qmd): fully cooperative play and asymmetric specialist roles, against an escalating outbreak that pushes back.
- [Paranoia](paranoia.qmd): adversarial-by-design play, a licensed-deceiver GM, and betrayal held inside a consent contract.
- [The Quiet Year](the-quiet-year.qmd): a GM-less community drawn onto a shared map, prompted by a deck of cards across four seasons.
- [Ten Candles](ten-candles.qmd): tragic horror played by literal candlelight, where ten extinguished candles are the clock and every character is doomed.
- [Thousand Year Old Vampire](thousand-year-old-vampire.qmd): solo journaling driven by prompts, where the diary is the game and memory is forced to decay.
- [Unlock!](unlock-series.qmd): a card-based boxed escape game with a companion app as judge, timer, and hint desk.

## Puzzle hunts

- [The MIT Mystery Hunt](mit-mystery-hunt.qmd): the founding puzzle hunt, where unmarked puzzles feed meta-puzzles and the winning team writes the next year's hunt.

## Live games

- [The Crystal Maze](crystal-maze.qmd): zoned timed challenges and theatrical hosting, paced across a long session.
- [Hunt A Killer](hunt-a-killer.qmd): a murder mystery serialised as monthly boxes of physical evidence to assemble.
- [The Mysterious Package Company](mysterious-package-company.qmd): a story delivered as real artefacts in the post, with no visible game layer.
- [Notable Escape Rooms](notable-real-escape-rooms.qmd): the live escape-room form, read through several landmark venues.
- [Sleep No More & Punchdrunk](sleep-no-more-and-punchdrunk.qmd): free-roaming immersive theatre, the mask contract, and the repeating loop as a multiple-visit engine.

## Digital games

Single-player video games that sit outside the guide's four craft-traditions but
illustrate its mechanisms cleanly, especially in puzzle design.

- [Baba Is You](baba-is-you.qmd): a puzzle game whose rules are tiles you rearrange, teaching its own grammar without a word.
- [Blue Prince](blue-prince.qmd): a roguelike of drafted rooms over a fixed mystery, where the only thing that persists between runs is what you understand.
- [The Witness](the-witness.qmd): an island of line-drawing panels that teach themselves in silence, until the world itself becomes the puzzle.
